update.description: Updates an existing pet resource identified by its ID. Accepts a Pet object with updated information (name, status, category, tags, etc.). Requires the pet ID to exist in the store. Returns the updated Pet object. All provided fields will be updated; partial updates are not supported.

Creates a new pet resource in the store. Accepts a Pet object with required fields (name, status) and optional fields (category, tags, photoUrls). Returns the newly created Pet object with an assigned ID. The pet is immediately available for queries and orders.

Retrieves a list of pets filtered by one or more status values. Accepts a comma-separated string of status values (e.g., 'available,pending,sold'). Returns an array of Pet objects matching the specified statuses. Useful for inventory management and order fulfillment workflows.

Retrieves a list of pets filtered by one or more tags. Accepts a comma-separated string of tag names (e.g., 'tag1,tag2,tag3'). Returns an array of Pet objects that have any of the specified tags. Useful for categorizing and discovering pets by custom attributes.

Retrieves a single pet resource by its unique ID. Requires a valid pet ID as a path parameter. Returns the complete Pet object including all properties (name, status, category, tags, photoUrls). Returns a 404 error if the pet ID does not exist.

Updates specific fields of an existing pet using form-encoded data. Accepts form parameters for pet ID (path), name, and status. Supports partial updates - only provided form fields are modified. Returns an ApiResponse object indicating success or failure. Useful for quick updates without sending full Pet objects.

Permanently removes a pet resource from the store by its ID. Requires a valid pet ID as a path parameter and optional API key authentication. The pet is immediately unavailable for queries and orders after deletion. Returns an ApiResponse object confirming the deletion. This operation cannot be undone.

Uploads an image file for a specific pet identified by petId. The image is associated with the pet's profile for identification and display purposes. Requires authentication. Returns an API response confirming successful upload with image metadata.

Retrieves the current inventory of pets in the store, organized by status (e.g., available, pending, sold). Returns a map where keys are pet status values and values are the count of pets in each status. Requires API key authentication. Useful for monitoring stock levels and availability.

Creates a new purchase order for one or more pets in the store. Accepts an Order object containing pet ID, quantity, shipment date, status, and completion flag. The order is persisted in the store system and assigned an order ID. Returns the created Order object with the assigned orderId. Side effect: Reduces available pet inventory based on ordered quantities.

Retrieves a specific purchase order from the store by its order ID. The orderId parameter must be a valid integer. Returns the complete Order object containing pet details, quantity, shipment date, status, and completion information. Note: This is a test endpoint with specific valid ID ranges (IDs <= 5 or > 10 return valid responses; other values may return errors).
update.summary: Retrieve purchase order details by ID

update.description: Deletes a purchase order from the store by its order ID. The orderId parameter must be a valid integer less than 1000. Upon successful deletion, the order is removed from the system and becomes unavailable for retrieval. Returns a success confirmation. Side effect: Inventory may be restored based on the deleted order's quantities if applicable.

Creates a new user account in the system. Accepts a User object containing username, email, password, first name, last name, address, city, state, zip, and phone. The user is persisted in the system with a unique user ID. Returns the created User object with assigned userId. Note: Authentication requirements depend on deployment context. Typically used for user registration or administrative user creation.

Creates multiple user accounts in a single operation by accepting an array of User objects. Each User object in the array should contain username, email, password, and other profile information. All users are persisted in the system with assigned user IDs. Returns a success confirmation with details of created users. This bulk operation is more efficient than creating users individually.

Authenticates a user and creates a session. Accepts username and password as query parameters. Returns a session token or authentication cookie on successful login. Sets up an authenticated session for subsequent API requests.

Terminates the authenticated user session. Requires valid authentication. Invalidates the current session token or authentication cookie, preventing further API requests with that session. Returns success confirmation.

Retrieves a user's complete profile information by username. Accepts a username path parameter. Returns the User object containing all user details including id, username, email, and other profile information. Does not require authentication.

Updates an existing user's profile information. Requires authentication and the authenticated user must match the username being updated. Accepts a User object in the request body with updated fields. Returns the updated User object. Supports partial updates - only provided fields will be modified.

Permanently deletes a user account. Requires authentication and the authenticated user must match the username being deleted. Accepts a username path parameter. Returns success confirmation. This operation is irreversible and removes all associated user data.
